item.title: Canada and Japan sign new strategic partnership, including military co-operation
item.description: On final stop of 10-day trip, PM Carney announces deeper ties with Japan in defence, energy, critical minerals and advanced technology such as artificial intelligence

item.title: ‘A foreign policy based on short memory’: Carney continues push to diversify from the U.S.
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney has moved away from a 'values-based' foreign policy to focus on the bottom line in an increasingly chaotic world.

item.title: Canada-U.S. trade talks are restarting. Here's what's at stake
item.description: For the first time since U.S. President Donald Trump called off negotiations last October — ostensibly over a TV ad — Prime Minister Mark Carney's point man on trade, Canada-U.S. Trade Minister Dominic LeBlanc, will meet face to face with his White House counterpart.

item.title: In Tokyo, Carney signs agreement with Japanese counterpart to expand trade and defence ties
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney inked a series of agreements with his Japanese counterpart Friday designed to expand and “modernize” the bilateral relationship as he continues his push to develop deeper ties to like-minded countries in the Indo-Pacific — now that the Canada-U.S. relationship is on shaky ground.

item.title: Carney to cap Indo-Pacific trip with Tokyo visit focused on trade, security
Ottawa office market experts left in the dark over feds’ return-to-work needs
item.description: OTTAWA - Ottawa real estate watchers are calling on the federal government to be more clear about its workspace needs as public servants prepa…

item.title: Man charged in connection with threat made against Premier Doug Ford: OPP
item.description: Police have charged a 20-year-old from Alliston in connection with allegedly making a threat against Premier Doug Ford.

item.title: Canada’s top soldier, European counterparts to discuss supporting Gulf states in Iran conflict
item.description: Any options that emerge from Friday morning meeting will be presented to Ottawa, Jennie Carignan says

item.title: Advocacy groups call on feds to review Alberta's Bill 11 health-care reforms
item.description: Doctors across Canada are "deeply concerned" about the Alberta government's plan to expand private health care, said Dr. Margot Burnell, president of the Canadian Medical Association — and they want Ottawa to step in.

item.title: Liberal MPs to hold call Friday as caucus raises concerns about Carney's shifting Iran position
item.description: Foreign Affairs Minister Anita Anand is set to brief Liberal MPs on Friday morning about the government's position on the war that has now engulfed Iran and surrounding countries in the Middle East, after some both privately and publicly raised concerns about Prime Minister Mark Carney's initial statement regarding the U.S.-Israeli strikes, CBC News has learned.

item.title: LeBlanc heads to Washington for renewed trade talks
item.description: Formal discussions resume after months of stalled negotiations

item.title: Canada could be called on to help defend Gulf states, says top military commander
item.description: The head of the Canadian Armed Forces says Canada may be called on to help defend Persian Gulf states from Iran's strikes as the U.S.-led offensive expands into a wider regional war.

item.title: Ontario formally takes over York Catholic School Board after threatening it was next
Ottawa says it still plans to pick one builder for new submarine fleet
item.description: It’s possible the government could split the contract once it has an opportunity to evaluate the bids, official says

item.title: Canadians assigned to 'other duties' outside of Iran strikes while on U.S. exchange: DND
item.description: The handful of Canadian military members serving on exchange with U.S. forces in the Middle East remain in their posts, but have been assigned "other duties" outside of the campaign against Iran, the Department of National Defence said.

item.title: Privacy Commissioner finds Loblaw took too long to delete PC Optimum accounts
item.description: Office says Loblaw took unreasonable amount of time to address requests from customers

item.title: Canada signing agreement with EU to enhance free trade deal
item.description: As relations with the Trump administration remain fraught and uncertain, trade ministers from Canada and the European Union are set to sign on to a series of improvements to their bilateral trade agreement.

item.title: Conservatives say any Iran military action should be up to Parliament
item.description: Conservative defence critic James Bezan says Parliament should have the final say on whether Canada deploys troops in a conflict and accuses Carney of being 'all over the map.'

item.title: Washington must avoid new tariffs with Ottawa: Canadian and U.S. mayors
item.description: Washington must avoid any new tariffs with Ottawa during a review of its trilateral trade agreement, mayors of several Canadian and U.S. cities urge.

item.title: LeBlanc heading to D.C. after Carney says CUSMA ‘broken in the short term’
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney said while in Australia that Canada's free trade pact with the U.S. 'effectively has been broken in the short-term by U.S. actions.'

item.title: Canada’s defence chief says allies may help Gulf states bombed by Iran
Mark Carney refuses to ‘categorically’ rule out military involvement in Middle East conflict
Canada’s multibillion-dollar mining gamble is testing its trade strategy
item.description: The postCanada’s multibillion-dollar mining gamble is testing its trade strategyappeared first onThe Logic.

item.title: The little we know about Canadian troops stationed in the Middle East
item.description: Here's what we know about Canada's involvement in the war launched this past weekend by U.S. President Donald Trump.

item.title: LeBlanc to meet Trump’s trade czar in Washington on Friday
Secretary of state says Canada not looking to split submarine contract
‘Buy Canadian’ policy likely to cost taxpayers $12 billion yearly: study
item.description: The study states that the 'Buy Canadian' policy, enacted in December 2025, could cost taxpayers $12 billion each year it is in effect.

item.title: Carney government working to bring 2,035 Canadians home from the Middle East, Anita Anand says
Mark Carney ties his own foreign policy in knots
item.description: Instead of reconciling principle and pragmatism, the PM made a mess of both

item.title: Mark Carney’s Iran reaction raises doubts about what he stands for
Doug Ford’s interest in Exhibition Place for new conference centre blindsides Toronto officials
Canada’s AI minister says OpenAI to change ChatGPT after Tumbler Ridge shooting
Airlines weigh risks in Mideast skies filled with 'hot pieces of flying metal'
item.description: Confusing advice and on-again-off-again airport closures are complicating the evacuation of hundreds of thousands of foreigners stranded in the conflict zone around the Persian Gulf. In a region that has seen tragic mistakes resulting in the deaths of hundreds of civilian air passengers, decisions on whether to fly remain fairly ad hoc and can come down to the judgment of an individual pilot.

item.title: Why parents are ‘livid’ over this TDSB survey about how to spend money
Frank Stronach’s defence asks for ‘directed verdicts’ on two charges as Crown closes its sex assault case
Canada hoping to charter flights for Canadians to leave U.A.E. in next 72 hours: Anand
item.description: Foreign Affairs Minister Anita Anand says the government is looking at getting Canadians out of the Middle East, including asking the United Arab Emirates to use its airspace for chartered evacuation flights.

item.title: OpenAI CEO expressed 'horror and responsibility' over ChatGPT's ties to Tumbler Ridge, AI minister says
item.description: Artificial Intelligence Minister Evan Solomon says OpenAI's CEO has agreed to let Canadian experts into its safety office to help evaluate future threats following the mass shooting in Tumbler Ridge, B.C.

item.title: Ottawa arranges charter, commercial flights to help evacuate Canadians from Middle East
item.description: Foreign Affairs Minister Anita Anand says she expects flights to leave for UAE in the next 72 hours

item.title: Carney won’t ‘rule out’ Canadian military involvement in Middle East
item.description: He suggests this would happen only under extraordinary circumstances and offers no indication any operations are under development

item.title: Carney tells Australian Parliament allies must draw closer as global order is 'breaking down'
item.description: In an address to Australia's Parliament that was something of a love letter to the country and its people, Prime Minister Mark Carney implored citizens in both Commonwealth realms to draw closer together amid a breakdown in the established global order.

item.title: ‘We’re not okay’: Calls for more support as Ontarians with intellectual disabilities face homelessness and exploitation
item.description: More Ontarians with intellectual and developmental disabilities are losing their homes and facing exploitation, creating, what advocates are calling, a system in crisis.

item.title: Michael Ford says he’s giving ‘serious consideration’ to Toronto mayoral run
item.description: Video

item.title: Ontario government to bring back paper report cards
item.description: Paper report cards are coming back to Ontario public schools, the Ministry of Education has confirmed.

item.title: Alberta 'excessively vulnerable' to foreign interference, experts warn
item.description: Alberta is not ready to deal with the threat of online disinformation coming from foreign actors in a possible referendum campaign on separation this fall, according to national security experts.

item.title: Arrests made at Toronto demonstration against changes to OSAP
item.description: Toronto police have arrested several people after a protest at Queen’s Park got heated Wednesday afternoon

item.title: Three charged, more than $2.3 million in suspected fentanyl seized in interprovincial police probe
How John Tory’s exit, some bad blood — and a Ford — are threatening to shake up Toronto’s mayoral race
item.description: As Brad Bradford moves closer to solidifying himself as the consensus centre-right mayoral challenger to progressive incumbent Olivia Chow, he…

item.title: Canadians want floor-crossing MPs to face ‘immediate’ byelections: poll
item.description: The Ipsos poll conducted for Global News found Canadians' displeasure with floor-crossing MPs has not hurt the overall approval for Prime Minister Mark Carney or the Liberals.

item.title: Rally over OSAP changes gets heated as protesters clash with police at Queen’s Park
Commandant of Canadian Army Command and Staff College in Kingston removed from post
item.description: Col. Fraser Auld's removal "follows a loss of trust in his ability to command," according to a Department of National Defence spokesperson.

item.title: Carney shakes up senior ranks of the public service
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney has shaken up the senior ranks of the public service, creating and expanding some roles in a continued effort to put his stamp on the machinery of government.

item.title: Moroun, Ambassador Bridge company turned to ex-Harper aides’ firm to lobby Canadian officials
item.description: The billionaire U.S. owners of the busiest land border crossing in North America turned to a high-powered lobbying firm run by former top aides to Prime Minister Stephen Harper in recent years, records show.

item.title: Michael Ford, premier’s nephew, considering run for Toronto mayor
How Canada’s position on the Iran war has evolved throughout the week
Carney supports U.S. strikes on Iran ‘with regret’ — what are other leaders saying?
OpenAI’s Sam Altman to meet AI minister, B.C. premier over safety changes
item.description: Evan Solomon sought the meeting after OpenAI said it would enhance its police referral threshold and other safety measures after fallout over the Tumbler Ridge mass shooting.

item.title: NATO must look ‘northward’ to Arctic threats, Anand warns in speech
item.description: Last month, Canada opened a consulate in Nuuk, Greenland, as U.S. President Donald Trump escalated his threats to acquire the semiautonomous territory of NATO ally Denmark.

item.title: Toronto to look at paying people to shovel sidewalks — just like New York City does
item.description: The city has struggled with its sidewalk plows, which have been notoriously unreliable.

item.title: Ottawa shuffles senior public service ranks
item.description: Announcement names 16 individuals who will be taking on new roles

item.title: John Tory decides not to seek re-election as Toronto’s mayor because of ‘inevitable attacks’
Anand says Ottawa working to help Canadians stranded in Middle East
item.description: There are no immediate plans to send military planes to the region to bring Canadians home, Foreign Affairs Minister says

item.title: Former Toronto mayor John Tory announces he won’t run in upcoming election
item.description: Former Toronto mayor John Tory says he will not seek the city’s top job in the fall municipal election.

item.title: Nova Scotia, Ontario alcohol deal garnering mixed reviews
item.description: A deal by Nova Scotia and Ontario to allow consumers to buy craft beer, wine and other alcoholic beverages directly from producers in each province is garnering mixed reviews.

item.title: Airlines suspend flights to Middle East as conflict between Iran and U.S., Israel intensifies
Why pumping more Canadian crude won’t stop oil prices from soaring
item.description: The postWhy pumping more Canadian crude won’t stop oil prices from soaringappeared first onThe Logic.

item.title: Federal government boosts scrutiny of businesses claiming to be Indigenous-owned
item.description: Gull-Masty stopped short of accepting the committee’s recommendation to put in place new eligibility and verification requirements for companies to be listed as Indigenous.

item.title: Critical minerals alliance with Australia an example of middle power strength, Carney says
item.description: Countries such as Canada and Australia have more power than they realize, especially in critical minerals, Prime Minister said in an address to Australia’s Lowy Institute

item.title: Immigration Department experimenting with AI to advise newcomers on where to settle
item.description: IRCC also using AI to detect fraud, including fake documents and morphed photographs

item.title: How Doug Ford got pulled into a fight over a Toronto hockey arena and youth centre — and why this faceoff may be ending
U.S. could hold a 'red card' over Canada's Gripen fighter jet option
item.description: As Ottawa reviews its F-35 purchase, there's concern Washington holds a powerful lever — control of the secure Link 16 network used for NATO and NORAD operations. Swedish Gripens and Danish F-35s operate together in NATO missions, but U.S. approval is required for the encryption system — raising concerns it could be withheld if Canada chooses the Gripen.

item.title: Saab sees Canada as 'great' partner to design next-generation fighter jets
item.description: Swedish company Saab says it's hoping to partner with the federal government and Canadian businesses to develop systems for the next generation of aerial warfare as it pursues deals to supply Ottawa with new aircraft.

item.title: 'It's not easy, let's be clear': Carney speaks frankly about dealing with Trump at an event in Australia
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney pulled back the curtain on his relationship with his American counterpart on Wednesday evening in Australia, saying it's not easy dealing with U.S. President Donald Trump and he must choose his words carefully to avoid any blowback.

item.title: NDP politicians set off on road trip to highlight northern Ontario road dangers
item.description: Three Ontario New Democrats have set off on a road trip from Toronto to the Manitoba border and back to highlight the dangers of northern roads.

item.title: Politics Insider: Anand asks Oman for use of country’s airspace to evacuate Canadians from Middle East
item.description: Ottawa’s top priority is helping the 100,000 Canadians who are stranded in the region, Foreign Affairs Minister said

item.title: Carney says he raised foreign interference concerns in ‘frank’ talk with Modi
item.description: Prime Minister declines to say whether conversation included India’s alleged role in Nijjar’s death

item.title: Carney says Ottawa’s position supporting U.S., Israeli strikes on Iran was taken ‘with regret’
item.description: The U.S. and Israel have acted without engaging the UN or allies, leaving the question of where things will go from here, Prime Minister says

item.title: Carney reaffirms Canada’s support for strikes on Iran ‘with regret’
item.description: Carney said in Australia that the growing Middle East conflict 'is another example of the failure of the international order' and that Canada's support 'is not a blank cheque.'

item.title: John Tory says he won’t run for mayor of Toronto
item.description: The former mayor resigned in 2023 after admitting to an extramarital affair

item.title: Ottawa asks Oman to use airspace to help evacuate Canadians from Middle East if necessary
item.description: Anand says the government’s top priority is helping the 100,000 citizens stranded in the region

item.title: John Tory in his own words: ‘What I’m asking for is your understanding’
item.description: John Tory's statement announcing his decision not to run for mayor in October.

item.title: Ontario speeds up review of critical minerals strategy
NDP politicians set off on road trip to highlight northern Ontario road dangers
item.description: TORONTO - Three Ontario New Democrats have set off on a 3,500-kilometre road trip from Toronto to the Manitoba boundary and back to highlight …

item.title: Toronto’s mayor wants to finally fix this foul-smelling city eyesore. It’s unclear how much it will cost
